heading: Civil action relating to disclosure of intimate images
label: 6851 Civil action relating to disclosure of intimate images

children.heading: Commercial pornographic content
children.label: 1 Commercial pornographic content
children.text: The term “commercial pornographic content” means any material that is subject to the record keeping requirements under .section 2257 of title 18

children.heading: Consent
children.label: 2 Consent
children.text: The term “consent” means an affirmative, conscious, and voluntary authorization made by the individual free from force, fraud, misrepresentation, or coercion.

children.heading: Depicted individual
children.label: 3 Depicted individual
children.text: The term “depicted individual” means an individual whose body appears in whole or in part in an intimate visual depiction and who is identifiable by virtue of the person’s face, likeness, or other distinguishing characteristic, such as a unique birthmark or other recognizable feature, or from information displayed in connection with the visual depiction.

children.heading: Disclose
children.label: 4 Disclose
children.text: The term “disclose” means to transfer, publish, distribute, or make accessible.

children.label: i
children.text: the uncovered genitals, pubic area, anus, or post-pubescent female nipple of an identifiable individual; or

children.label: I
children.text: on to any part of the body of an identifiable individual;

children.label: II
children.text: from the body of an identifiable individual; or

children.label: III
children.text: an identifiable individual engaging in sexually explicit conduct and1So in original.1

children.label: i
children.text: voluntarily display the content depicted; or

children.label: ii
children.text: consent to the sexual conduct depicted.

children.heading: Sexually explicit conduct
children.label: 6 Sexually explicit conduct
children.text: The term “sexually explicit conduct” has the meaning given the term in subparagraphs (A) and (B) of .section 2256(2) of title 18

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (4), an individual whose intimate visual depiction is disclosed, in or affecting interstate or foreign commerce or using any means or facility of interstate or foreign commerce, without the consent of the individual, where such disclosure was made by a person who knows that, or recklessly disregards whether, the individual has not consented to such disclosure, may bring a civil action against that person in an appropriate district court of the United States for relief as set forth in paragraph (3).

children.heading: Rights on behalf of certain individuals
children.label: B Rights on behalf of certain individuals
children.text: In the case of an individual who is under 18 years of age, incompetent, incapacitated, or deceased, the legal guardian of the individual or representative of the identifiable individual’s estate, another family member, or any other person appointed as suitable by the court, may assume the identifiable individual’s’ rights under this section, but in no event shall the defendant be named as such representative or guardian.1

children.label: A
children.text: the fact that the individual consented to the creation of the depiction shall not establish that the person consented to its distribution; and

children.label: B
children.text: the fact that the individual disclosed the intimate visual depiction to someone else shall not establish that the person consented to the further disclosure of the intimate visual depiction by the person alleged to have violated paragraph (1).

children.label: i
children.text: an individual may recover the actual damages sustained by the individual or liquidated damages in the amount of $150,000, and the cost of the action, including reasonable attorney’s fees and other litigation costs reasonably incurred; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the court may, in addition to any other relief available at law, order equitable relief, including a temporary restraining order, a preliminary injunction, or a permanent injunction ordering the defendant to cease display or disclosure of the visual depiction.

children.heading: Preservation of anonymity
children.label: B Preservation of anonymity
children.text: In ordering relief under subparagraph (A), the court may grant injunctive relief maintaining the confidentiality of a plaintiff using a pseudonym.

children.label: A
children.text: an intimate image that is commercial pornographic content, unless that content was produced by force, fraud, misrepresentation, or coercion of the depicted individual;

children.label: i
children.text: to a law enforcement officer or agency;

children.label: ii
children.text: as part of a legal proceeding;

children.label: iii
children.text: as part of medical education, diagnosis, or treatment; or

children.label: I
children.text: unlawful content; or

children.label: II
children.text: unsolicited or unwelcome conduct;

children.label: C
children.text: a matter of public concern or public interest; or

children.label: D
children.text: a disclosure reasonably intended to assist the identifiable individual.
